get.description: The data export API is an efficient API for exporting data. It will only return "washed/cleaned" data, that is data that matches official skills, industries etc. that you have defined in the Masterdata tool. It returns data in batches of 100. Each response contains a link to the next batch (if there are more). Customers and Users contains ids that refer to the ids of industries, skills etc. so that you should take into consideration that some of the data sets are dependent on other data sets.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Select Flowcase defined data export set. Valid values are only - industries, skills, customers, custom_tag_categories and users.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Default/max limit is 100, use offset parameter to fetch next bulk of users.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Default/max limit is 100, use offset parameter to fetch next bulk of users.

post.description:
The CV report can be generated using the following steps: 1. Retrieve Office IDs 2. Initiate the report using this endpoint and note report Id. 3. Poll the report status until complete by using endpoint - Retrieve Report Request with noted report Id. 4. When complete, download the report There is a limit of 5000 CVs per result, so you can use the filtering on offices to fetch the result in appropriate size batches.
Report Type Description
user_report Information about users
usage_report Information about when a CV was last updated
project_experiences CV data - project experiences
certifications CV data - certifications
courses CV data - courses
languages CV data - languages
technologies CV data - skills and skill categories
key_qualifications CV data - summaries
educations CV data - educations
work_experiences CV data - work experiences
positions CV data - positions

parameters.name: output_format
parameters.description: Choose output format for either XLSX or CSV

parameters.name: report_type
parameters.description: Select report from list

get.description: Provide ReportId returned by "Initiate Report Request" endpoint

parameters.name: reportId
parameters.description: Report Id from returned by "Initiate Report Request" endpoint

get.description: Masterdata can be retrieved from the following API endpoint.

parameters.name: include_categories
parameters.description:
The include_categories parameter determines whether the masterdata's categories will be
included in the result. Note: only skills (technologies/tags) can have categories.

application/json.example:
An example response is shown below.
{
"_id": "5a9019390e3b410f4fa10000",
"id": "5a9019390e3b410f4fa10000",
"section_type": "[SECTION]",
"field_name": "[FIELD]",
"values": { "int": "Name" },
"external_unique_id": null,
"help_text": {},
"category_ids": ["5f3155e46c292a116ba80000"],
"child_count": 0,
// only included if `include_categories` parameter is 'true'
"categories": [
{
"_id": "6a9019390e3b410f4fa10000",
"id": "6a9019390e3b410f4fa10000",
"section_type": "[SECTION]",
"field_name": "[FIELD]",
"values": { "int": "Category Name" },
"external_unique_id": null,
"help_text": {},
"category_ids": [],
"child_count": 1,
}
]
}

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description:
Change the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results.
e.g. To get the first page of 20 results:
GET https://servicehub.atea.com/cv-partner/masterdata/{section}/{field}?offset=0&limit=20
and to get the next page set the offset to the number of the result to start from:https://servicehub.atea.com/cv-partner/masterdata/{section}/{field}?offset=20&limit=20

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Change the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results.
e.g. To get the first page of 20 results:
GET https://servicehub.atea.com/cv-partner/masterdata/{section}/{field}?offset=0&limit=20
and to get the next page set the offset to the number of the result to start from:https://servicehub.atea.com/cv-partner/masterdata/{section}/{field}?offset=20&limit=20

parameters.name: include_categories
parameters.description: The include_categories parameter determines whether the masterdata's categories will be included in the result.
